You have to categorize your data because let's say you have leads that are 90% of your database and you have 10% of your database that's customers. Well if you market to the 10% of people that are customers like they're a lead you're typically going to disrespect them by marketing to them for something that they've already purchased and that's going to annoy them and that's going to slow down their ability to increase their lifetime customer value with you through time because you're not marketing to them the right way. You're not creating context with them, you're not respecting them because you're not even showing them that you care to tag them in your database for a particular type of person that they are to you and your company to then market to them appropriately. ## [03:01 - 06:01]

Joe down the road who they don't even know. How effective of a conversation with that unless you're a great story type. Adding a tag is very important. It creates context and the ability to have context in your conversations. Removing a tag, maybe you accidentally added some tags, you got too excited. Bringing somebody to a sequence. Keep in mind a sequence is a series of automation separated by time created by you and the actions that occur in that sequence could be subscribing them or unsubscribing them to specific tools. Could be sending them to different third party softwares through tools like Zapier. It could also be a series of messages walking them through a series of communications that you want to market to this person. You could also unsubscribe people from a sequence in bulkier. You can set subscriber custom fields. Custom fields keep in mind are just additional data sources if you will on a contact record that you can then add in to create more context. Let's say everybody that's on your list, let's say you have an Excel sheet that has the same names of these people from a different database that adds a new field into your mini chats fields so you can add more context to the conversations. Let's say that in mini chats since you don't have the ability to use their emails, what if you exported your database and you have all the emails now in the names of your customers and then you exported your mini chat database so you had a name and you have two Excel sheets now. Let's say you put those in the same Excel sheet, you ran a duplicate checker, you just data sorted them that way everybody lined up with the same contact record that they are. After you had everybody lined up and you had all the same data, what if you added the emails of these people that are in your mini chats or added the phone numbers or added whatever specific piece of data you have that you don't have in mini chat on them yet to their mini chat record because then in the mini chat bot automation that you create, you can use that specific custom field that you've uploaded to maybe get a new updated email record and then use Zappier to update that database that you have pulled the older email that's no longer in use down to whatever creative limitation you put on yourself to use this specific tool. It doesn't necessarily look as effective if you just say to yourself, oh it's a custom field what can I do with that. You can do a ton with that but use the idea more or less around updating this database from your other databases that you have or that your clients have.
